description_long: This cowshed is a large, rectangular agricultural building with a low-pitched, hipped roof featuring a prominent raised central section for ventilation. The exterior walls are constructed from a combination of dark metal or wood panels and a lower brick or masonry base, providing both durability and insulation. The structure is supported by a series of evenly spaced columns along the perimeter, creating open bays that likely serve as entry points or for airflow. The roof includes a long, narrow ridge vent running along the top, enhancing internal ventilation for livestock. There are no visible windows, but the open bays and roof vent suggest a focus on air circulation. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and robustness for housing cattle in an urban or peri-urban setting.
description: A large, rectangular cowshed with a hipped roof, brick base, dark paneled walls, and open bays supported by columns. The structure features a raised central roof vent for enhanced ventilation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with open bays and supporting columns, as well as the raised central roof vent.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long expanse of the building, multiple open bays, and the continuous ridge vent along the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with open bays and columns visible, but no prominent entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the length of the building, open bays, and roof vent.
attribute_door_style: wide open bays with possible sliding or hinged metal doors
attribute_window_presence: no windows; open bays provide light and air
